title: contour3
summary: Create 3-D contour line plots in MATLAB and RunMat.

gpu_support.notes: `contour3` uses the shared contour-generation path and can keep GPU-resident Z inputs on device through the WGPU plotting pipeline.

description: `contour3` creates 3-D contour line plots from scalar fields. It accepts core `contour`-style inputs but places each isoline at its scalar Z level rather than projecting onto a flat plane.
behaviors: `contour3(Z)` uses implicit X and Y axes derived from the Z matrix shape.
`contour3(X, Y, Z)` accepts explicit vector axes or meshgrid-style X and Y matrices.
A scalar fourth argument selects the number of contour levels; a vector fourth argument selects exact contour levels.
LineSpec color tokens such as `'k'` and name-value pairs such as `'LineWidth'` are accepted.
GPU-aware contour generation is used when the shared plotting path can operate directly on GPU-resident scalar fields.
options: `'LevelList'` / `'Levels'` accepts an explicit, strictly increasing vector of contour values.
`'LevelStep'` specifies the spacing between generated contour levels.
`'LineColor'` accepts MATLAB color strings, RGB triples, `'auto'`, and `'none'`.

examples.description: Use explicit contour levels with high-contrast lines

faqs.question: How is contour3 different from contour?
faqs.answer: `contour` draws contour lines on a single flat plane. `contour3` places each contour line at its own scalar level on the Z axis, which makes level height visible in the 3-D camera.

faqs.question: Can contour3 be combined with surface plots?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use `hold on` to overlay `contour3` with `surf`, `mesh`, `plot3`, or `scatter3`. Because `contour3` is camera-aware, it shares the same 3-D axes state as other 3-D plot types.

gpu_residency: `contour3` preserves GPU residency when the shared WGPU plotting context can consume the Z buffer directly. If that path is unavailable, RunMat gathers once and builds the same contour geometry on the CPU.
